Fungal infection.desc: Fungal infection caused by invading fungi taking over skin, nails, or scalp.

Allergy.desc: Immune system reaction to foreign substances like pollen, food, or pet dander.

GERD.desc: Stomach acid frequently flows back into the esophagus causing irritation and heartburn.

Chronic cholestasis.desc: Liver condition where bile flow is reduced or blocked causing itching and jaundice.

Drug Reaction.desc: Adverse reaction to medication ranging from mild rash to severe anaphylaxis.

Peptic ulcer diseae.desc: Sores on the lining of the stomach or the upper small intestine.

AIDS.desc: Advanced HIV infection severely damaging the immune system.

Diabetes.desc: Metabolic disease causing high blood sugar due to insulin deficiency or resistance.

Gastroenteritis.desc: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines causing vomiting and diarrhea.

Bronchial Asthma.desc: Chronic condition where airways narrow and swell producing excess mucus.

Hypertension.desc: Persistently high blood pressure that can lead to heart disease and stroke.

Migraine.desc: Neurological condition causing intense headaches often with nausea and light sensitivity.

Cervical spondylosis.desc: Age-related wear on spinal disks in the neck causing pain and stiffness.

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).desc: Loss of muscle function caused by bleeding in the brain — medical emergency.

Jaundice.desc: Yellowing of skin and eyes caused by excess bilirubin in the blood.

Malaria.desc: Mosquito-borne disease caused by Plasmodium parasites transmitted via Anopheles mosquitoes.

Chicken pox.desc: Highly contagious viral infection causing itchy blister-like rash across the body.

Dengue.desc: Mosquito-borne viral infection causing high fever, rash, and severe joint pain.

Typhoid.desc: Bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi spread through contaminated food or water.

hepatitis A.desc: Highly contagious liver infection caused by Hepatitis A virus via contaminated food.

Hepatitis B.desc: Serious liver infection caused by Hepatitis B virus transmitted through bodily fluids.

Hepatitis C.desc: Viral infection causing liver inflammation sometimes leading to serious liver damage.

Hepatitis D.desc: Liver disease caused by Hepatitis D virus that only occurs alongside Hepatitis B.

Hepatitis E.desc: Liver disease caused by Hepatitis E virus spread through contaminated water.

Alcoholic hepatitis.desc: Liver inflammation caused by heavy alcohol consumption over extended time.

Tuberculosis.desc: Serious bacterial infection mainly affecting lungs caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

Common Cold.desc: Viral infection of the upper respiratory tract causing runny nose and sore throat.

Pneumonia.desc: Infection inflaming air sacs in one or both lungs which may fill with fluid.

Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).desc: Swollen veins in the rectum or anus causing pain, bleeding, and discomfort.

Heart attack.desc: Blockage of blood flow to the heart muscle — critical medical emergency.

Varicose veins.desc: Enlarged twisted veins appearing in legs due to faulty valves.

Hypothyroidism.desc: Underactive thyroid gland failing to produce enough thyroid hormone.

Hyperthyroidism.desc: Overactive thyroid producing too much thyroxine accelerating metabolism.

Hypoglycemia.desc: Abnormally low blood sugar causing shakiness, confusion, and dizziness.

Osteoarthritis.desc: Degeneration of joint cartilage and bone causing pain and stiffness.

Arthritis.desc: Inflammation of one or more joints causing pain and reduced movement.

(vertigo) Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo.desc: Inner ear disorder causing brief episodes of intense dizziness.

Acne.desc: Skin condition causing pimples, blackheads, and whiteheads on face and body.

Urinary tract infection.desc: Infection in any part of the urinary system most commonly affecting the bladder.

Psoriasis.desc: Skin disease causing red itchy scaly patches most commonly on scalp and elbows.

Impetigo.desc: Highly contagious bacterial skin infection causing red sores that burst and crust.
